name: Q1349491
abstract: Carl Donnell "Kansas" Fields (December 5, 1915, Chapman, Kansas – March 7, 1995, Chicago, Illinois) was an American jazz drummer. Fields played in Chicago from the late 1920s, and worked with King Kolax and Jimmie Noone in the 1930s.

about_quotes.speaker: Big Chielf Moore
about_quotes.evidence: In the band I had Buck Clayton, the drummer this time was Kansas Fields... and we had a nice engagement in Columbus.

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Burrell
about_quotes.evidence: and the drummer—the replacement drummer was from Chicago—his name was Kansas Fields, and myself and Dizzy.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Burrell — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: A drummer by the name of Kansas Fields, Carl Fields is his name, we called him Kansas. He left here Sidney Bechet with Cindy Shea. Dave Young, tenor player that worked with Roy at the Three Deuces, and Rozelle Claxton, beautiful piano player.

about_quotes.speaker: David Albert Francis
about_quotes.evidence: I heard from Cab Calloway -- a drummer that was with him, Kansas Fields... he was looking for a drummer... that's when I joined the band

about_quotes.speaker: Percy Heath
about_quotes.evidence: And J.C. Heard’s brother, David, Kansas Fields. I’m talking about five different drummers while I was there a year and a half.
about_quotes.doc_title: Percy Heath — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: I met his drummer whose name was Kansas Field. I became better acquainted with Kansas later when he came to New York.
